channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What Americans think about Hegseth, Gabbard and key Trump Cabinet picks: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As several of President-elect Donald Trump’s choices for high-level positions in his incoming administration face scrutiny on Capitol Hill, a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research shows that Americans have their own doubts.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top GOP rebel angles for powerful House leadership-backed committee post
item.description: Rep. Chip Roy is floating himself as a possible candidate to chair the influential House Rules Committee next year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tren de Aragua are ideological terrorists disguised as a street gang warns former military officer
item.description: A former high-ranking Venezuelan military officer is sounding the alarm about the migrant gang Tren de Aragua being used as a tool of the Venezuelan government to sow violence and discord throughout the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mitt Romney's Senate exit may create a vacuum of vocal, conservative Trump critics
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — With Mitt Romney set to exit the U.S. Senate, Washington will be without one of its strongest conservative critics of Donald Trump when the president retakes the White House in the new year.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watchdog finds FBI intelligence missteps before Jan. 6 riot, but no undercover agents were present
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI should have done more to gather intelligence before the Capitol riot, according to a watchdog report Thursday that also said no undercover FBI employees were on the scene on Jan. 6, 2021, and none of the bureau’s informants was authorized to participate.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump offers support for dockworkers union by saying ports shouldn't install more automated systems
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is offering his support for the dockworkers union before their contract expires next month at Eastern and Gulf Coast ports.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden's full list of clemency and commutation recipients revealed
item.description: The White House on Thursday unveiled a list of names of the 39 individuals whom President Biden pardoned, as well as a list of the nearly 1,500 individuals whose sentences he commuted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC to fund guaranteed income program: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City will provide funding for a guaranteed income program to support soon-to-be mothers experiencing housing insecurity.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: More than 20 days into phenomenon, Pentagon still has no answers about origins of mysterious NJ drones
item.description: More than three weeks after dozens of mysterious drones began popping up over New Jersey, the public has still been offered no clear insight on what the phenomenon could be.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Senate chaplain Barry Black hospitalized after brain bleed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. Senate Chaplain Barry Black has been hospitalized after suffering a bleed on his brain and is expected to have a “smooth recovery,” his office said.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: Turning Tides
item.description: The Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Conservative group lists 'woke radical leftists' it wants fired from DOJ under Pam Bondi
item.description: A conservative research group has written to President-elect Trump’s Attorney General nominee Pam Bondi, calling on her to fire a number of Justice Department workers who it says are “woke radical leftists and donors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House: No evidence of public safety threat from NJ drone sightings
item.description: The White House on Thursday said there is no evidence of a national security or public safety threat after reports of drone sightings in New Jersey, highlighting that the government is working to identify what the mysterious sightings are.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz says he’ll propose anti-fraud measures, reflects on VP bid
item.description: In his first interview with MPR News since his vice presidential run, Tim Walz said he still has “privilege” of serving as Minnesota governor, urged lawmakers to work together in divided government.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Conservative group debuts major ad buy in key senators' states as 'soft appeal' for Hegseth, Gabbard, Patel
item.description: The initial ad buy from Heritage Action for America focused on Pete Hegseth, but the group says it is interested in appealing to senators in favor of several other nominees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-FBI informant accused of lying about Bidens agrees to plead guilty to federal charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A former FBI informant accused of falsely claiming that President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepted bribes has agreed to plead guilty to federal charges, according to court papers filed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Youngkin to draft sanctuary city ban, making state funding contingent on ICE cooperation
item.description: Virginia Gov. Glenn Youngkin will announce a plan to institute a sanctuary city ban and withhold state funds for policing to such jurisdictions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Military service academies see drop in reported sexual assaults after alarming surge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Reported sexual assaults at the U.S. military service academies dropped in 2024 for the second year in a row, according to new Pentagon data, marking a sharp turnaround from an alarming surge two years ago that triggered sweeping reviews and an overhaul in leadership.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 things to know about Biden’s 1,500 clemencies, pardons
item.description: President Biden pardoned 39 people and commuted the sentences of nearly 1,500 others on Thursday, a sweeping move that comes after pressure from criminal justice advocates over the surprise pardon of his son Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden administration makes final diplomatic push for stability across a turbulent Mideast
item.description: Top U.S. officials are in the Middle East pressing for stability in Syria and an end to Israel’s 14-month war in the Gaza Strip in a last-ditch diplomatic push by the outgoing Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Josef Federman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China denies new report linking CCP to four sites in Cuba allegedly used to spy on the US
item.description: The Center for Strategic and International Studies released a report last week detailing facilities in Cuba that it claims China may be using to gather signal intelligence on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Trump attorney lashes out at 'lawfare' after court appearance in Wisconsin fake electors case
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s former Wisconsin attorney has lashed out at the state’s Democratic attorney general for filing felony charges against him and two others related to the 2020 fake electors scheme.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House passes bill to add 66 new federal judgeships, but prospects murky after Biden veto threat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — What was once a bipartisan effort to expand by 66 the number of federal district judgeships across the country passed the House of Representatives on Thursday, though prospects for becoming law are murky after Republicans opted to bring the measure to the floor only after President
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell working from home after tripping at Capitol
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) missed his usual morning speech to open the Senate on Thursday after falling earlier in the week and suffering a minor cut to the face and a sprained wrist.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House approves mammoth annual defense policy bill
item.description: The House passed the annual defense policy bill Wednesday, sending the mammoth measure to the Senate for consideration ahead of Congress’s end-of-month deadline.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: House sends Biden bill adding judicial seats after veto threat
item.description: The House approved a bill Thursday that would create dozens of additional judicial seats over the next several years, sending it to President Biden’s desk after his administration levied a veto threat.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FAA administrator to resign on Trump swearing-in
item.description: Mike Whitaker is leaving at the start of President-elect Trump’s term, a source familiar with his plans told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tells TIME he will pardon Jan. 6 rioters on day 1
item.description: The President-elect, who will serve his second term as the first American president to be convicted of a felony, was named TIME's 2024 Person of the Year.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed 'Trump' law to allow felons to run for office in Missouri
item.description: The "Donald J. Trump Election Qualification Act" would grant the possibility of those who have been guilty of a felony charge to run.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Judge to decide fate of Trump’s New York hush money conviction
item.description: The fate of President-elect Trump’s New York hush money case is now in the hands of Judge Juan Merchan, who oversaw the historic first conviction of the former and now incoming president.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Future of Democratic Party rest in hands of one of these candidates
item.description: While the average American may not know who the chair of the Democratic National Committee is, the role has a direct impact on their lives.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Rising US-Mexico tension a game of optics, analyst says
item.description: Change is coming to the border on Jan. 20, when President-elect Trump takes office. How profound and lasting such change might be remains to be seen, but one thing is certain: Optics will play a big role in Washington, D.C., and in Mexico City.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Florida state lawmaker files measure eliminating tuition aid for undocumented immigrants
item.description: Florida state Sen. Randy Fine (R) announced Monday he was introducing a bill to limit college tuition aid to undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: As session nears, status of a Minnesota lawmaker facing criminal charges remains a flashpoint
item.description: Sen. Nicole Mitchell has upcoming court hearings in a burglary charge ahead of a trial that would start soon after 2025 Legislature gavels in. She’s resisting calls to step down, and a departure would leave the Senate knotted up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump-era Justice Department subpoenaed congressional staffers, watchdog finds
item.description: But the department's internal watchdog found no evidence of political motivation by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court rejects challenge to Boston's school admissions policy
item.description: The court's action was the second time the justices declined to intervene in an admissions program based on geography since their 2023 ruling invalidating affirmative action in higher education.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News
